event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>CatVideoFest 2026</show-title>
event.headline: Watch cat videos, paint your own cat lamp
event.description: It’s true what they say: cat people are craft people. At our Crafthouse screening, a live host (almost certainly covered in cat hair) will guide you through painting your own cat lamp while CATVIDEOFEST 2026 plays on the big screen. Craft from your seat with the lights up, then take your masterpiece home. No litter box required.

It’ll be like summer camp for horror fans.

show.title: Butthole Surfers: The Hole Truth and Nothing Butt
show.headline: The story of like-minded misfits who embarked on a radical rock journey

landscapeHeroImage.altText: BUTTHOLE SURFERS: THE HOLE TRUTH AND NOTHING BUTT

portraitHeroImage.altText: BUTTHOLE SURFERS: THE HOLE TRUTH AND NOTHING BUTT

posterImages.altText: BUTTHOLE SURFERS: THE HOLE TRUTH AND NOTHING BUTT

event.title: <show-title>Butthole Surfers: The Hole Truth and Nothing Butt</show-title><event-type-title> with Livestream Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Watch the Butthole Surfers streaming live from Austin
event.description:
The story of one of Austin’s wildest, weirdest, and most uncompromising bands comes to the big screen followed by a Q&A broadcast live from Alamo Drafthouse South Lamar in Austin, TX.

Director Tom Stern joins Butthole Surfers members Paul Leary, King Coffey, Jeff Pinkus, and Gibby Haynes for a conversation moderated by musician Bob Forrest. Together, they’ll dig into the band’s four-decade history, its infamously chaotic live shows, and the making of a documentary every bit as unruly as its subject.

show.title: Elf
show.headline: The best way to spread Christmas cheer

event.title: <show-title>Elf</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A cheerful celebration with themed games, props & singing loud for all to hear
event.description:
"Buddy the Elf, what's your favorite color?"

Don’t be a cotton-headed ninnymuggins this holiday season. Come out to Alamo Drafthouse and spread the cheer at this sugar-fueled, interactive holiday spectacular, packed with games, props, and, of course, singing loud for all to hear.

Bundle up for this celebration of one of the best holiday films ever made, period. Will Ferrell’s take on a North Pole elf trying to figure out Manhattan (“Watch out! The yellow ones don’t stop!”) just might be the funniest he’s ever been.

Our ELF Movie Party features all sorts of fun props, a gift "wrap off" before the film, and of course, your very own elf hat.

image.altText: Elf Summer Movie Party Web Movie Party Deluxe Showpage 1920x1080 copy

contentBlocks.paragraphs: Whether it’s maple syrup or another refreshing beverage, it’ll go down better in our exclusive ELF 16 oz. collector’s glass. Featuring the movie logo on one side and the phrase “Cotton-headed Ninny-muggins” on the other, this pint glass is available for a limited time on AlamoMart.com. Order now and proudly celebrate Christmas in July at the ELF Movie Party.

show.title: The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Start your engines

event.title: <show-title>The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A NOS-fueled party for family
event.description: Join us for a high-octane movie party celebrating the 25th anniversary of the original FAST movie. Before the cars in Tokyo drifted, before they went to space, and before any Vin Diesel feuds, it was just a simple undercover cop story about stolen VCRs, street racing, and loyalty tested at 100 miles per hour. Strap in for this special screening with a live host, exclusive themed keepsakes like a bumper magnet, bottle opener, and a NOS keychain, as well as a theater full of family.

event.title: <show-title>Six: The Musical Live!</show-title> <event-type-title>Sing-Along</event-type-title>
event.headline: Belt out the hits from the West End smash
event.description:
Get ready to lose your head at this special sing-along screening of SIX: THE MUSICAL LIVE! This is your chance to rule the room alongside the queens and sing along with every iconic number in a theater full of fellow Tudorphiles.

Please note that our usual No Talking/No Singing Showtunes policy is relaxed for this screening, but please be reasonable and save a little vocal power for the rest of the monarchy.

show.title: Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town

event.title: <show-title>Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town
event.description:
They promised to reunite 10 years later, at 9:30, because McKinley had something at 11 and he’d already moved it twice. Now it’s 25 years later, and Camp Firewood is back in session and on the big screen. Exclusively at Alamo, we’re pairing this special anniversary party screening with a themed menu that promises more than canned vegetables, a custom camp T-shirt, and other surprises. Whether you’ve spent the last 25 years wondering if you taste like burger or just trying to make it to the campfire before somebody breaks into song, this is your chance to come back to Camp Firewood. We want you inside us.

Guests who purchase tickets after July 20th will receive a voucher to redeem their shirt on AlamoMart instead of receiving it in theater.

event.title: <event-type-title>Advance Screening:</event-type-title> <show-title>Tony</show-title>
event.headline: Get a taste of it early with an exclusive menu
event.description: Join us for an advance screening of TONY and see Matt Johnson's moving, funny, and deeply human Anthony Bourdain origin story before it officially hits theaters. We're also offering an exclusive menu inspired by some of Bourdain's favorite indulgences, including a double-decker hamburger ("a ballistic missile of protein delivery”), the Guilty Attraction Fries with mac & cheese and chicken bites, plus a Negroni – the "satanic delicious hell-broth" Bourdain could never resist. It's the closest thing to having Tony order for the table.

show.title: Little Shop of Horrors (1986)
show.headline: The iconic black comedy horror musical – back on the big screen

event.title: <show-title>Little Shop of Horrors (1986)</show-title>
event.headline: Oz, Ashman, and Menken’s iconically queer monster movie musical turns 40!
event.description:
Feed me, Seymour. Feed me all night long.

Alarm goes off at seven, and you start uptown. You put in your eight hours for the powers that have always been. Ugh, ain’t that the truth, Chiffon?

It’s been a hot minute since we’ve screened a musical for Queer Film Theory 101, so in honor of its 40th anniversary, we’re showing the melodic and incendiary critique of hustle culture, LITTLE SHOP OF HORRORS, from powerhouse writing duo Howard Ashman and Alan Menken and director Frank Oz. Well ahead of its time, this film tackles hot topics like urban decay, abusive dentistry, aggressively sexual plants, and presages the rise of influencer culture.

When dopey orphan-turned-beleaguered florist’s assistant Seymour (Rick Moranis) stumbles upon an exceptionally strange and exotic plant, he suddenly sees a way out of Skid Row for himself and the equally troubled object of his affection, Audrey (Ellen Green). Unfortunately for everyone involved, Seymour’s leafy meal ticket, Audrey II (Levi Stubbs), harbors murderous desires of its own, revealing itself as an extraterrestrial invader hell-bent on human domination.

Featuring an all-star cast including Steve Martin, John Candy, Tisha Campbell, and Bill Murray, unforgettable songs from Ashman and Menken, and incredible puppetry from Lyle Conway (THE DARK CRYSTAL, The Muppet Show), LITTLE SHOP OF HORRORS is perhaps the perfect movie musical. Don’t argue with us. Deeply queer, filled with critiques of capitalism, and catchy as hell, we can’t wait to celebrate this iconic musical's 40th anniversary with y’all as part of Queer Film Theory 101. And just a gentle reminder: don’t feed the plants!

show.title: Rashomon
show.headline: The husband, the wife...or the bandit?

show.title: The Piano
show.headline: A tale of lust and seduction

event.title: <show-title>The Piano</show-title>
event.headline: Holly Hunter’s ultimate mute sad girl performance won her a much deserved Oscar
event.description:
THE PIANO centers on a mute Scottish woman, Ada McGrath (Holly Hunter), who is shipped off to rural New Zealand to marry settler Alisdair Stewart (Sam Neill). She travels with the two most important things in her life: her out-of-wedlock daughter (Anna Paquin) and the ornate piano she uses to express her feelings through. When her new husband sells her piano to a frontiersman (Harvey Keitel), Ada finds herself in an unexpected affair that leads to dark consequences.

THE PIANO earned three Academy Awards: one for Hunter, one for Paquin, and one for Jane Campion (THE POWER OF THE DOG) for best screenplay. A gentle but sometimes harsh film, THE PIANO is a stunning, complex romance not unlike WUTHERING HEIGHTS. It feels deeply rooted in its time period and organic in its storytelling. Keitel and Hunter’s chemistry is lightning – a smouldering sensation that radiates through the screen. The romance Campion directs is profoundly messy, and because of that messiness, it’s profoundly beautiful.

show.title: Divine Hammer
show.headline: Usher in a new era of death

event.title: <show-title>Divine Hammer</show-title>
event.headline: Usher in a new era of death
event.description:
Two maladapted young women, both part of the same internet gore enthusiast community, formulate a plan to meet in person with the intent that one will kill the other, film the killing, and then sell the cameras that “watched” to unsuspecting customers online in the hopes of spurring on a “new era of death.”

Inspired in equal parts by mumblecore, shot-on-video horror, and a deep unease formed by the internet’s mass syndication of violence, DIVINE HAMMER is the debut feature by the M. Sisters, the multimedia duo of Hazel and Mae M.

show.title: Michael Mann's Manhunter: The Final Cut
show.headline: New 4K restoration of Michael Mann's final cut

event.title: <show-title>Michael Mann's Manhunter: The Final Cut</show-title>
event.headline: New 4K restoration of Michael Mann's final cut
event.description:
A retired criminal profiler (William Petersen) reluctantly returns to the FBI to help track down a serial killer of families and is forced to consult an expert – the imprisoned Dr. Hannibal Lecktor (Brian Cox in a subtly chilling performance). The third feature written and directed by Michael Mann is a gripping adaptation of the first of Thomas Harris' four novels featuring the iconic murderer Lecktor, and benefits from the filmmaker's typically striking visuals as well as a top-notch supporting cast, including Tom Noonan as the hunted killer and three-time Oscar nominee Joan Allen as his blind love interest.

This new 4K final cut, overseen by Mann, enhances the film’s depth, visual clarity and sound, offering audiences the ultimate immersive version. Shot in 1985, MANHUNTER was on a frontier in both its subject matter and film form, initiating an impressive list of subsequent films and series. (Rialto)

show.title: Full Contact (1992)
show.headline: HK action hottie Chow Yun-Fat in Ringo Lam’s '90s sleazefest!

event.title: <show-title>Full Contact (1992)</show-title>
event.headline: HK action hottie Chow Yun-Fat in Ringo Lam’s '90s sleazefest!
event.description: The ultimate ‘90s Hong Kong action sleazefest by bullet balletmaster Ringo Lam (CITY ON FIRE, PRISON ON FIRE). Chow Yun-Fat (CROUCHING TIGER, HIDDEN DRAGON) steps into the most gleefully tasteless shoot-em-up of his storied career. Queer arms dealers, fields of exploding automobiles, harsh neon greens and blues, madness with butterfly knives, biker punk chic, ripping donuts on motorbikes and heaping helpings of horniess, oh my!

event.title: <show-title>Pure Scum</show-title>
event.headline: A hidden gem straight from SXSW Sydney
event.description: Two recently graduated private schoolboys are involved in a serious car accident after a party and flee into the city to escape the consequences of their actions. Knowing they will eventually be caught, they decide to bury their troubles in hedonistic pursuits of alcohol, drugs and women. Before long, a series of dangerous decisions sends the night spiraling into violence with terrifying speed.

show.title: Boyhood
show.headline: A film twelve years in the making

event.title: <show-title>Boyhood</show-title> <event-type-title>w/Pre-Recorded Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Featuring a pre-recorded Q&A with the cast and crew
event.description:
Featuring a pre-recorded Q&A with director Richard Linklater and cast members Ellar Coltrane, Patricia Arquette, and Ethan Hawke.

Texas filmmaker Richard Linklater has made some of the best independent films of the last three decades. BOYHOOD might be his masterpiece.

Shot over twelve years with the same cast, BOYHOOD tracks the childhood of Mason, a kid who struggles to find his place in a family that's drifting apart, in a state that's wider than most countries. As the film captures the small moments - both tragedies and triumphs of growing up - audiences will watch the actors age on screen. Ellar Coltrane, Patricia Arquette, Ethan Hawke, and Lorelei Linklater star.

BOYHOOD is a funny, humane, and - most important - essential portrait of life, as only the art of cinema can capture.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: All Ages
sessionAttributes.description: For all ages shows, we welcome babies and kids of all ages. The no talking rules are relaxed, since kids will be kids. But other than that, you can expect the full Alamo experience.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.

agePolicies.name: All Ages
agePolicies.description: All minors under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Children ages 6+ admitted. Children 5 and under (including infants) are ONLY admitted during specifically designated "All Ages" and "Family Friendly" screenings.
